location.descriptionAr: Unknown — requires official or field verification.

historicalSummary.en: Founded by Ahmad ibn Tulun for his new administrative city al-Qata'i. It is one of the oldest and largest surviving Islamic monuments in Cairo and preserves a major early Islamic architectural language.
historicalSummary.ar: يتطلب النص العربي الكامل مراجعة محرر متخصص قبل النشر العام.

architectureSummary.en: A vast open courtyard is surrounded by four arcades; the outer ziyada separates the mosque from the city. Brick construction, pointed arches, stucco ornament, and the spiral minaret define the monument.
architectureSummary.ar: يتطلب النص العربي الكامل مراجعة محرر متخصص قبل النشر العام.

stories.summaryEn: The place belongs to Cairo's layered historic urban and cultural landscape; the record's public status is controlled by the inventory label.
stories.summaryAr: ينتمي المكان إلى المشهد العمراني والثقافي التاريخي متعدد الطبقات في القاهرة؛ وتحدد بطاقة الجرد وضعه العام.

visitorInformation.currentStatus: Unknown — requires official or field verification.

claims.textEn: The inventory identifies this record as Mosque of Ahmad Ibn Tulun.
claims.textAr: يسجل الجرد هذا الموقع باسم جامع أحمد بن طولون.

claims.textEn: Founded by Ahmad ibn Tulun for his new administrative city al-Qata'i. It is one of the oldest and largest surviving Islamic monuments in Cairo and preserves a major early Islamic architectural language.
claims.textAr: يتطلب النص العربي الكامل مراجعة محرر متخصص قبل النشر العام.

claims.textEn: A vast open courtyard is surrounded by four arcades; the outer ziyada separates the mosque from the city. Brick construction, pointed arches, stucco ornament, and the spiral minaret define the monument.
claims.textAr: يتطلب النص العربي الكامل مراجعة محرر متخصص قبل النشر العام.

claims.textEn: Current visitor information is not asserted without a current official notice.
claims.textAr: لا تُثبت معلومات الزيارة الحالية دون إعلان رسمي حديث.
